title: Same Parity Swaps in Binary Strings

content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN (i.e. every character of SSS is either 000 or 111).

You can perform the following operation on SSS:


select any two indices i,ji, ji,j of the same parity, i.e. either both i,ji, ji,j are odd or both i,ji, ji,j are even
swap SiS_iSi​ and SjS_jSj​
For example, in the string 111011101110, we can swap the second and the fourth characters to get 101110111011. However, we can never obtain 110111011101 from 111011101110 by performing such swaps.

Find the maximum possible number of occurrences of the string 010101 as a substring of SSS after performing the above operation any number of times (it is also allowed to not perform any operation).

For example, the string 111011101110 has no occurrence of the string 010101 as a substring, whereas we can swap the second and fourth characters to obtain 10111{\color{red}{01}}11011 which has exactly one occurrence of 010101 (colored red).


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. The description of the TTT testcases follow.
Each testcase consists of two lines.
The first line contains a single integer NNN, the length of the string SSS.
The second line contains a binary string of length NNN.


Output Format

For each testcase, print in a single line, an integer — the answer as per the problem statement.
constraints: The sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all testcases doesn't exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only strings that can be obtained by performing the given operations are {10000,00100,00001}\{10000, 00100, 00001\}{10000,00100,00001}. Of these the two strings 001000{\color{red}{01}}0000100 and 00001000{\color{red}{01}}00001 contain exactly one occurrence of 010101.
Test case 222: The given string SSS cannot be changed by performing the given operation and contains 222 occurrences of the string 010101, i.e. 01010{\color{red}01}{\color{blue}01}001010.
Test case 333: The only strings that can be obtained by performing the given operations are {00101,10001,10100}\{00101, 10001, 10100\}{00101,10001,10100}. The string 001010{\color{red}{01}}{\color{blue}{01}}00101 contains two occurrences of 010101.
